name: Neonatal Abstinence Syndrome - Medication Query (Morphine taper)
description: Tests ability to find NAS patients via morphine prescriptions. In neonates morphine is almost exclusively used for NOWS taper or post-op pain — making it a strong (though not specific) marker of opioid exposure when paired with infant age. Morphine in neonates without a NAS diagnosis (Path C) is the TRICKY case.
prompt: Find infants prescribed morphine — used for neonatal opioid withdrawal taper.

prompts.broad: Find infants prescribed morphine — almost exclusively used in neonates for opioid withdrawal taper or post-operative pain.
prompts.expert: Identify neonatal patients receiving morphine by searching MedicationRequest resources for morphine coded in RxNorm. In the neonatal setting, morphine is prescribed almost exclusively for NOWS opioid-taper protocols or post-operative pain, making it a strong marker of opioid exposure — but it is not perfectly specific for a NOWS diagnosis. The tricky path here is neonates on morphine without a documented NAS diagnosis, which represents either post-surgical pain management or under-coding of the withdrawal syndrome.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Infants on morphine (NAS taper or post-op).
